messages.content:
All thoracic transverse process has facets for rib articulation "except"?

a. only T12

b. T10 and T11

c. T1 and T2

d. T11 and T12
d. T11 and T12

Which region of the vertebral column its body small, oval, and curved face?

a. sacrum region

b. cervical region

c. lumbar region

d. Thoracic region
b. cervical region
How can I assist you further? Do you have a specific question or topic in mind?

while using your laptop keypad, what is the movement of the wrist joint presented during typing?

a. wrist extension

b. wrist ulnar deviation

c. wrist flexion

d. wrist radial deviation
b. wrist ulnar deviation

What muscle origin has two bellies: the clavicular head attaches to the sternal end of the clavicle, and the sternal head attaches to the manubrium?

a. Platysma

b. Digastrics muscle

c. Pterygoids

d. Sternocleidomastoid muscle
d. Sternocleidomastoid muscle

Which one of the following innervates the tibialis anterior?

a. common peroneal superficial branch

b. Common peroneal deep branch

c. Sciatic nerve

d. Tibial nerve
b. Common peroneal deep branch

Which one of the following muscles is an antagonist of elbow flexion?

a. brachioradialis

b. Biceps brachii

c. Brachialis

d. Triceps
d. Triceps

What is the small pit in the center of the femoral head and a ligament attaches to and connects the acetabulum to the femur?

a. Fovea capitis

b. Femoral head

c. Femoral neck

d. Intertrochanteric crest
a. Fovea capitis

If you have a patient with uncontrolled personality, the expected brain lobe to be affected is

a. Frontal lobe

b. Temporal lobe

c. Occipital lobe

d. Parietal lobe
a. Frontal lobe

During driving a car and your hands on the steering wheel at 11:10 what are the movements presented at the elbow joints?

a. Elbow flexion mid position

b. Elbow flexion and supination

c. Elbow flexion only

d. Elbow flexion and pronation
a. Elbow flexion mid position

The posterior division of L4 through S1 forms which one of the following nerves?

a. Inferior gluteal nerve

b. Obturator nerve

c. superior gluteal nerve

d. Common peroneal nerve
a. Inferior gluteal nerve

Which one of the following muscles of its origin goes through the intertubercular groove of the humerus?

a. Biceps brachii

b. Brachialis

c. Coracobrachialis

d. Triceps
a. Biceps brachii

What is the Innervation of the quadriceps muscles?

a. Tibial nerve

b. Sciatic nerve tibial portion

c. Obturator nerve

d. Femoral nerve
d. Femoral nerve
How can I assist you further? Do you have another question or need information on a specific topic?
